label: Leukaemia
quick_take: Leukaemia in CI5 has one massive outlier — **Zimbabwe Bulawayo African women, vol II, ASR 42.5** — which is 8× the world median and stands well above every registry. Alongside that, the dataset shows clear Kuwaiti paediatric signals and remarkable stability in age distributions across decades.

findings.title: Zimbabwe Bulawayo African women vol II: ASR 42.52
findings.text: extraordinary for 1960s-era African data. Almost certainly HIV-associated *before* HIV was named (lymphoid leukaemia / lymphoma overlap), but the magnitude is striking regardless. Sits as the #1 outlier for the site.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Zimbabwe Bulawayo African women vol II: ASR 42.52

findings.title: India Trivandrum men: the by-now-familiar U-shape
findings.text: 21.93 (vol VII) → 3.75 (vol VIII) → 5.46 (vol XII). Vol VII is real (it is the very highest Asian male leukaemia rate recorded); the dive to vol VIII is a scope change.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — India Trivandrum men: the by-now-familiar U-shape

findings.title: Kuwait non-Kuwaitis women vol IX: early-peak at age band 1 (0–4), ASR at peak 38.7
findings.text: paediatric leukaemia signal elevated by the large migrant-worker demographics (young population skew).
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Kuwait non-Kuwaitis women vol IX: early-peak at age band 1 (0–4), ASR at peak 38.7

findings.title: Bimodal age curves in India Tamil Nadu, Korea, Hong Kong, Chinese cities
findings.text: a clear paediatric peak (band 1–3) *and* an older-adult peak (band 14–17). This is the "two leukaemias" pattern (ALL in childhood + AML/CLL in late adulthood) showing up geographically wherever the registry is big enough to record both.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Bimodal age curves in India Tamil Nadu, Korea, Hong Kong, Chinese cities

findings.title: US ethnic contrasts 5× or less
findings.text: unusually small for a cancer site. Leukaemia is one of the more ethnically balanced cancers in CI5.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — US ethnic contrasts 5× or less

findings.title: France Calvados men: U-shape recovery
findings.text: 4.31 → 3.27 → 13.29. Pronounced rise, U-shape with a significant rebound.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — France Calvados men: U-shape recovery

findings.text: Leukaemia aggregates ALL + AML + CML + CLL — each with very different age patterns. Cross-registry comparison without sub-type split is limited.
Zimbabwe Bulawayo vol II (1963–67) is based on small absolute numbers; the ASR of 42 is credible but the magnitude should be stated with wide uncertainty.

The **Zimbabwe Bulawayo 1960s female leukaemia outlier** needs a historical retrospective — what was captured at Mpilo Hospital then, and is it really ALL/AML or a lymphoma-leukaemia misclassification?
A CI5-wide "two-peak fingerprint" visualisation (paediatric + old-age) would show how well large registries can capture both sub-populations and how many smaller registries cannot.
Kuwait non-Kuwaiti paediatric peak could be used to illustrate population-demography effects on crude age curves.
